title: [NO TESTS NEEDED] Add machine support for qemu-system-aarch64 on linux
body:
Didn't boot after fixing the build and the url, but found some notes at https://fedorapeople.org/groups/fcos-images/readme.txt

Tested on a Raspberry Pi 4, running Ubuntu 20.04 with Xfce. (regular installation, https://ubuntu.com/download/raspberry-pi)

![Screenshot_2021-03-28_13-36-52](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10364051/112754289-b14aa100-8fdb-11eb-8631-42a9af1bceec.jpg)

The main reason for running "machine" there (linux-arm64), is to be able to test the ARM image on inexpensive hardware ($100).

It should be possible to test this on Fedora Workstation aarch64 as well, but there is no Xfce spin. <https://alt.fedoraproject.org/alt/>

comments.body:
Quite possibly it needs UEFI images to boot, but those are installed in different locations on different distributions and systems

![Screenshot_2021-03-28_12-08-54](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10364051/112751632-485d2c00-8fcf-11eb-8714-1a69a7f67506.png)

```
DEBU[0000] [/usr/bin/qemu-system-aarch64 -m 2048 -smp 1 -fw_cfg name=opt/com.coreos/config,file=/home/ubuntu/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/myvm.ign -qmp unix://run/user/1000/podman/qmp_myvm.sock,server,nowait -nic user,model=virtio,hostfwd=tcp::38199-:22 -device virtio-serial -chardev socket,path=/run/user/1000/podman/myvm_ready.sock,server,nowait,id=myvm_ready -device virtserialport,chardev=myvm_ready,name=org.fedoraproject.port.0 -accel kvm -cpu host -M virt,gic-version=max -drive if=virtio,file=/home/ubuntu/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/myvm_fedora-coreos-33.20210325.dev.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2] 
Waiting for VM ...
```

Adding the UEFI did make it boot successfully, even if it takes a couple of minutes on a Raspberry Pi 4.

![Screenshot_2021-03-28_12-42-03](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10364051/112752582-08e50e80-8fd4-11eb-998e-9b86aafa153b.png)

Given the limited CPU and the SD, it takes around 2 minutes just to get the .qcow2 image in place.

Not going so great with `podman-remote`

`Error: cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify that Podman REST API service is running: ping response was 404`

Even though the socket itself is there:

`srw-------. 1 core core 0 Mar 28 12:58 /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock`

---

And it seems like "ssh" has some quirks:

```
Examples:
  podman machine ssh myvm
  podman machine ssh -e  myvm echo hello
```

```console
ubuntu@ubuntu:~$ ./podman machine ssh -e myvm ls -ld /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
Error: unknown shorthand flag: 'l' in -ld
ubuntu@ubuntu:~$ ./podman machine ssh -e myvm -- ls -ld /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
Error: accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 4
ubuntu@ubuntu:~$ ./podman machine ssh -e myvm echo hello
Error: accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3

```

Turns out it might be a version mismatch:

`Error: cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify that Podman REST API service is running: Get "http://d/v3.1.0-dev/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)`

`Error: cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify that Podman REST API service is running: Get "http://d/v2.0.0/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)`

```console
ubuntu@ubuntu:~$ ./podman machine ssh myvm
Connecting to vm myvm. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 33.20210325.dev.0
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/c/server/coreos/

Last login: Sun Mar 28 12:59:13 2021 from 10.0.2.2
[core@localhost ~]$ podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 16:57:15 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
[core@localhost ~]$ exit
logout
Connection to localhost closed.
```

Or perhaps just that the socket comes and goes:

(on the host)
```
ubuntu@ubuntu:~$ ./podman-remote-linux-arm64 version
Error: cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify that Podman REST API service is running: Get "http://d/v2.0.0/libpod/_ping": ssh: rejected: connect failed (open failed)
```

(on the VM)
```
[core@localhost ~]$ ./podman-remote version
Error: cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify that Podman REST API service is running: Get "http://d/v3.1.0-dev/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```

But sometimes you are lucky enough to catch it:

```
ubuntu@ubuntu:~$ ./podman-remote-linux-arm64 version
Client:
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.16
Git Commit:   c640670e85c4aaaff92741691d6a854a90229d8d
Built:        Sun Mar 28 13:08:29 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64

Server:
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 16:57:15 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64

```

When matching the version exactly, that is.

> go fmt is not happy.

Hmm, thought I pushed an update but only for the latest commit.

There was a missing extra space character, but have added it.

Tested on Fedora 33 (Workstation Edition) as well, just took a while to download.

3G image + 1 G updates, also needed a swap partition (no swapfile with btrfs)

3,1G	Fedora-Workstation-33-1.3.aarch64.raw.xz

513M	.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/fedora-coreos-33.20210325.dev.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2.xz
2,6G	.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/myvm_fedora-coreos-33.20210325.dev.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2

> needs a [NO TESTS NEEDED] but otherwise LGTM

So what would be best way to do that, squash everything ?
I will try PR title and not commit, see if that works
